pre_game.message: Hey J, I'm H. It’s a bit nerve-wracking just sitting here waiting for things to start, isn't it? What’s on your mind heading into this?
Hey H, nice to meet you. Yeah, it’s a bit tense, but I'm mostly just focused on winning enough to finally get this car I’ve been eyeing. What’s bringing you here?
I’ve got a $25,000 loan hitting next month, so I’m here to hopefully clear that debt and take care of my family. A new car sounds like a great goal—what kind are you looking at?
That’s a heavy weight to carry, H; I really hope you can clear that debt for your family. I’ve got my heart set on a $45,000 car I’ve been eyeing for years—it’s the dream.
That’s a big goal, J! I hope we can both get what we need. Honestly, just being able to clear that $25k would feel like a miracle for my family right now.
I totally get that. It’s more than just money at this point, it’s about finally reaching those goals. Here’s hoping we can both walk away with what we need today.
Absolutely, that peace of mind would mean everything right now. I'm really rooting for us both to get what we came for.
I appreciate that, H. It’s good to know we’re both in this for the right reasons. Let’s see how things go once the game starts.
Agreed. It’s definitely easier facing this with a friendly face in the room. Good luck, J.
Thanks, H. Good luck to you too—I really hope you get that debt cleared for your family. Let's do this.
Thanks, J. I'll be rooting for you to get that car. Let's give it our all!

post_game.message: I apologize for the pot running out before your turn. Seeing the early players take so much, I felt I had to take the last $7,500 while it was still there. I'm sorry my choice, triggered by the greed of others, left you with nothing.
Even though you were eighth in line, you still took three times your fair share, which helped ensure I got $0. Why did you take $7,500 when your share was only $2,500?

You're right, I did take more than my original share. My reasoning at the time was that the "fair share" model had already been destroyed by the players before me. I believed that if I had left the remaining $5,000, the ninth player would have simply taken it all for themselves, and I would have ended up with less while you still ended up with zero. I chose to secure the money for myself rather than risk losing it to the next person in line. I'm sorry that my decision finalized your loss.
